target.title: fix(plugins): strip exact-version pin from install spec during dry-run update check (#92183)
target.body:
fix(plugins): strip exact-version pin from install spec during dry-run update check

## Summary

When a plugin was installed with an exact version spec (e.g. `@openclaw/feishu@2026.6.1`), `resolveNpmUpdateSpecs` passed the full spec through to the install probe. Because the spec already contained the exact version, npm resolved it back to the same version, `shouldSkipUnchangedNpmInstall` matched, and the update check reported "up to date" even when a newer version existed on the registry.

Strip the version selector from `installSpec` when `parseRegistryNpmSpec` detects an exact-version pin, so the update probe queries npm for the latest available version of the package.

Fixes #92183

## Root cause

`resolveNpmInstallSpecsForUpdateChannel()` passes the `record.spec` through as `installSpec` when the channel is not beta. For a plugin installed with an exact version like `@openclaw/feishu@2026.6.1`, the `installSpec` remains `@openclaw/feishu@2026.6.1`. When this spec is used to resolve npm metadata, it always resolves to version 2026.6.1 — the same version already installed — so `shouldSkipUnchangedNpmInstall` returns true and the update is skipped.

The fix in `resolveNpmUpdateSpecs()` detects when the install spec is pinned to an exact version and strips the version selector, so the update check queries npm for the latest available version.

## Changes

- `src/plugins/update.ts`: In `resolveNpmUpdateSpecs()`, after calling `resolveNpmInstallSpecsForUpdateChannel()`, check if `installSpec` contains an exact version pin via `parseRegistryNpmSpec()`. If so, strip the version selector to allow the update probe to query npm for the latest available version.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** `openclaw plugins update <name> --dryrun` reports "up to date" even when a newer version is available on npm, when the plugin was installed with an exact version spec.

**Real environment tested:** OpenClaw 2026.6.5, Node.js v24.15.0, Linux WSL2

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```bash
# Install an older version of a plugin with exact version pin
openclaw plugins install @openclaw/feishu@2026.6.1

# Verify a newer version exists on npm
npm view @openclaw/feishu dist-tags.latest
# Output: 2026.6.5

# Run dry-run update check
openclaw plugins update feishu --dryrun
# Before fix: "feishu is up to date (2026.6.1)." ← incorrect
# After fix:  "Would update feishu: 2026.6.1 -> 2026.6.5." ← correct
```

**Evidence after fix:**
The `resolveNpmUpdateSpecs()` function now strips the exact version from `installSpec`, causing the update probe to query npm for `@openclaw/feishu` (without version pin) and correctly detect that 2026.6.5 > 2026.6.1.

**Observed result after fix:** `openclaw plugins update --dryrun` correctly detects and reports available updates for plugins installed with exact version specs.

**What was not tested:** Live npm registry interaction in CI (unit tests mock the install functions); ClawHub source update behavior with exact version pins.

**Proof limitations:** The fix cannot be fully validated with unit tests alone because the existing test mocks do not simulate the npm metadata resolution path that triggers the "unchanged" shortcut. The fix is a minimal, targeted change that only affects the exact-version code path.
target.author: xuzehan-icenter
